titleStmt.title: Letter from James McFadden Brown to Inspector General, Neville Francis Fitzgerald Chamberlain, 5 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James M'Fadyen Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by James McFadden Brown (b.1879), a clerk at the Royal Irish Constabulary office, to the inspector general. The letter is Brown's reply to the Chief Secretary of Ireland, Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), requiring information about Brown's whereabouts from 24 April to 1 May 1916. Brown states that he attempted to report to the office after hearing that Dublin Castle had been taken by the rebels.The letter was addressed to Inspector General Neville Francis Fitzgerald Chamberlain (1856-1944). Chamberlain held the position from 1900 until his resignation in July 1916, making him the second longest serving inspector general in the force's history.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/24041916/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
R.I. Constabulary Office. Dublin Castle. The Inspector General, In reply to the Chief Secretary's
communication of 1st inst regarding my
movements, etc. , from noon on 24.4.16 to
noon on 1.5.16, I beg to report as follows: - 22.4.16. On duty at R.I.C. Office until 1 p.m.
Returned to private residence (Seapoint House ,
Monkstown) until 4 p.m. Spent the evening
in the City. 23.4.16. Spent in Monkstown & immediate
vicinity 24.4.16. - Do. - 25.4.16. Went to the City to report at the Office
but heard the Castle was held by the
rebels. Returned to Monkstown at 3 p.m.
for remainder of the day. 26.4.16 Went to the City but was informed
by Mr Cooney of R.I.C. Office that no
business was being transacted. Returned
to Monkstown about 3 p.m. for remainder of the day. 27.4.16 Spent in Monkstown and immediate vicinity. 28.4.16. - Do. - 29.4.16.Went to the City but informed by
Mr Cooney of R.I.C. Office that no
business was being transacted. Returned
to Monkstown about 1 p.m. for remainder of the day. 30.4.16. Spent in Monkstown and immediate
vicinity. 1.5.16. - Do. - On each of these nights I slept at
BlackrockSeapoint House, Monkstown. Jas. Mc F Brown 5th May '16.
